title: Missing Main Return Type
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The Main method must be static. Without the 'static' modifier, it won't be recognized as the entry point.

title: String Comparison Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: String comparison with == is case-sensitive in C#. 'hello' != 'HELLO'. Use string.Equals(a, b, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) for case-insensitive comparison.

title: Struct Default Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Before C# 10, structs cannot have explicit parameterless constructors. In C# 10+, this is allowed but may surprise developers expecting default(Point) behavior.

title: Null Reference on Value Type
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot implicitly convert int? (nullable) to int. Use x.Value (throws if null), x ?? defaultValue, or explicit cast.

title: Switch Fallthrough
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: C# does not allow implicit fallthrough between switch cases. Each case must end with break, return, goto, or throw.

title: Property Not Initialized
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Name is null by default (reference type). Calling .Length on null throws NullReferenceException. Initialize Name or check for null first.

title: Readonly Field Modification
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Readonly fields can only be assigned in the declaration or in a constructor. Modifying them in a method is a compile error.

title: Abstract Class Instantiation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot create an instance of an abstract class. You must create a derived class that implements all abstract members.

title: Missing Override Keyword
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Without 'override', Dog.Speak() hides the base method instead of overriding it. Polymorphism won't work correctly. Use 'override' to properly override.

title: Enum Comparison with Integer
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot directly compare enum with int in C#. Use (int)c == 0 or c == (Color)0, or compare with the enum value directly: c == Color.Red.

title: Foreach Modification
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Modifying a collection during foreach iteration throws InvalidOperationException. Use a for loop in reverse, or build a separate list of items to remove.

title: Interface Method Not Implemented
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: ConsoleLogger implements ILogger but doesn't implement LogError. All interface members must be implemented.

title: Async Void Event Handler
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: async void methods cannot be awaited and exceptions are unobservable. Use async Task instead. async void is only acceptable for event handlers.

title: Boxing Unboxing Mismatch
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Unboxing must match the original boxed type exactly. boxed contains an int, not a long. Use (int)boxed first, then cast to long.

title: String Equality vs Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: When strings are typed as 'object', == compares references, not values. a and b may be different objects. Equals() does value comparison. Output: False then True.

title: Disposing Without Using
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: StreamReader implements IDisposable but is never disposed. File handle leaks. Use 'using var reader = ...' or 'using (var reader = ...) { }'.

title: Static Class Instance Method
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Static classes can only contain static members. The method 'Add' must be declared static.

title: Virtual Method in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Calling virtual methods in constructor is dangerous. When Base() calls Initialize(), Derived's override runs but _name is still null, causing NullReferenceException.

title: Event Without Null Check
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: If no handlers are subscribed, Clicked is null. Invoking it throws NullReferenceException. Use Clicked?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty).

title: Comparison Operator on Struct
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Structs don't have == operator by default. You must implement operator == and != for custom structs.

title: LINQ Deferred Execution
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: LINQ queries use deferred execution. evens.Count() is evaluated when called, after 4 was added. Count returns 2 (both 2 and 4), not the expected 1.

title: Captured Loop Variable
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The lambda captures the variable 'i', not its value. When executed, all lambdas see i=5 (final value). Output: '55555'. Capture a local copy inside the loop.

title: Incorrect Dispose Pattern
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The Dispose method doesn't actually release any resources. It should follow the Dispose(bool) pattern and release unmanaged resources in the disposing path.

title: Mutable Struct Property
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Mutable structs are dangerous. When a struct is passed by value, Increment modifies a copy. While this specific code works, using mutable structs in collections or properties leads to subtle bugs.

title: Async Task Not Awaited
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: ComputeAsync returns Task<int>, not int. Without await, 'result' is a Task object, not 42. The program prints the Task's type name instead of the computed value.

title: String Interpolation
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: String interpolation with $ prefix is valid C# 6+. Expressions inside {} are evaluated and inserted into the string.

title: Null Conditional Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The ?. operator returns null if name is null, and ?? provides a default. Output: 0. This is safe null handling.

title: Auto-Property Initializer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Auto-property initializers (C# 6) set default values without a constructor. Output: 'Unknown'.

title: Pattern Matching
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Switch expressions with pattern matching (C# 8) provide concise type-based branching. The 'when' clause adds guards.

title: LINQ Query Syntax
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: LINQ query syntax is valid C#. This filters even numbers and squares them. Output: '4 16 36'.

title: Tuple Deconstruction
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Named tuples and deconstruction are valid C# 7. The method returns a named tuple that's deconstructed in the caller.

title: Async Await
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: async Main (C# 7.1) allows top-level await. The method correctly returns Task and awaits the async operation.

title: Record Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Records (C# 9) provide value-based equality and built-in ToString. p1 == p2 is true (same values). p1.ToString() shows 'Point { X = 1, Y = 2 }'.

title: Extension Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Extension methods add functionality to existing types. The 'this' keyword before the first parameter enables calling it as an instance method.

title: Nullable Reference Types
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nullable reference types (C# 8) with #nullable enable provide compile-time null checking. string? explicitly states the variable can be null.

title: Init-Only Property
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Init-only setters (C# 9) allow setting properties only during object initialization. After construction, properties are immutable.

title: Using Declaration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Using declarations (C# 8) dispose the resource at the end of the enclosing scope. Cleaner than using blocks.

title: Delegate Syntax
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Func and Action delegates with lambda expressions are valid C#. Func returns a value, Action returns void.

title: Top-Level Statements
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Top-level statements (C# 9) allow writing code without a Main method. The compiler generates the entry point automatically.

title: Generic Constraint
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Generic constraints 'class' (reference type) and 'new()' (parameterless constructor) are valid. The Create method can safely use new T().

title: Dictionary Initializer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Index initializer syntax (C# 6) allows using [] in object initializers. This is equivalent to dict["one"] = 1 after construction.

title: Span<T> Usage
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Span<T> provides a memory-safe view of contiguous data without copying. slice contains {2, 3, 4}.

title: Readonly Struct
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Readonly structs (C# 7.2) guarantee immutability. All fields/properties must be readonly. This avoids defensive copies in readonly contexts.

title: Local Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Local functions (C# 7) can be recursive and capture surrounding variables. Factorial computes 120.

title: Expression-Bodied Members
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Expression-bodied members (C# 6/7) use => for concise single-expression implementations in constructors, properties, and methods.

title: Deconstruct Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Deconstruct methods (C# 7) allow tuple-style deconstruction of any class. The method returns values via out parameters.

title: Index and Range
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Index (^) and Range (..) operators (C# 8) provide Python-like slicing. ^1 is the last element (5), 1..4 gives {1, 2, 3}.

title: Discard Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Discards (_) allow ignoring values you don't need. The string part of the tuple is discarded. This is valid C# 7.

title: Target-Typed New
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Target-typed new expressions (C# 9) omit the type when it can be inferred from the variable declaration.

title: Raw String Literals
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Raw string literals (C# 11) use triple quotes. They preserve formatting and don't require escaping quotes. Indentation is trimmed based on closing quotes.

title: Task.Result Deadlock
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Calling .Result on an async task in a synchronous context can cause a deadlock (especially in UI/ASP.NET). Use await or GetAwaiter().GetResult() with caution.

title: Equals Without GetHashCode
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Overriding Equals without overriding GetHashCode causes incorrect behavior in hash-based collections (Dictionary, HashSet). Equal objects must have equal hash codes.

title: Incorrect Lock Object
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Locking on 'this' is bad practice because external code can also lock on the same instance, causing deadlocks. Use a private readonly object for locking.

title: String Builder Misuse
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: String concatenation in a loop creates a new string object each iteration (O(n²) time). Use StringBuilder.Append() for efficient string building.

title: IEnumerable Multiple Enumeration
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Each call to Count(), First(), and foreach enumerates the source. If data comes from a database query or network, this causes multiple trips. Materialize with .ToList() first.

title: Async Lambda Without Await
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Task.Delay(1000) returns a Task but it's not awaited. The delay is ignored and 'End' prints immediately after 'Start'. Add 'await' before Task.Delay.

title: Struct Copy Semantics
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Structs are passed by value. DoubleVolume modifies a copy, not the original. settings.Volume is still 50. Pass by ref to modify the original.

title: Implicit Interface Implementation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Both interfaces have a Name property. A single implicit implementation serves both, which may not be intended. Use explicit interface implementation to differentiate.

title: Generic Covariance Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: List<Dog> cannot be assigned to List<Animal>. Generic classes are invariant in C#. If allowed, adding an Animal to animals would corrupt the Dog collection.

title: ConfigureAwait Missing
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: In library code, not using ConfigureAwait(false) unnecessarily captures the synchronization context, which can cause deadlocks and performance issues.

title: Float Precision Issue
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 0.1f is a float literal, which has precision issues. When added to a decimal, the float imprecision carries over. Use 0.1m for decimal precision.

title: Singleton Not Thread-Safe
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The null check and assignment are not atomic. In a multi-threaded environment, two threads can create separate instances. Use Lazy<T> or double-checked locking.

title: Incorrect Equality Override
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Equals only compares Amount, ignoring Currency. new Money(100, "USD").Equals(new Money(100, "EUR")) returns true, which is semantically wrong.

title: Deadlock with Two Locks
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Method1 locks lock1 then lock2. Method2 locks lock2 then lock1. Running concurrently causes classic deadlock. Always acquire locks in a consistent order.

title: Async in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Calling .Result in a constructor can deadlock (especially with sync contexts). Use a factory method pattern: static async Task<DataService> CreateAsync().

title: Type Cast Without Check
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Direct cast (string)obj throws InvalidCastException when obj is not a string. Use 'as' operator with null check, or 'is' pattern matching.

title: Disposable Not Disposed in Collection
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Clear() removes items from the list but doesn't Dispose them. MemoryStream implements IDisposable. Iterate and dispose each stream before clearing.

title: Wrong Exception Rethrow
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 'throw ex' resets the stack trace, losing the original error location. Use 'throw;' (without the variable) to preserve the original stack trace.

title: LINQ OrderBy Then Where
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While correct in output, ordering before filtering is inefficient. Filter first with Where(), then sort the smaller set with OrderBy() for better performance.

title: Sealed Override Without Base Call
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Derived.OnLoad doesn't call base.OnLoad(). While syntactically valid, skipping the base call can break the initialization chain if Base.OnLoad has required logic.

title: Thread Safety in Property
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The check-then-act pattern is not atomic. Between checking Value < max and Value++, another thread can modify Value. Use Interlocked or lock.

title: Dynamic Type Pitfall
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After reassigning x to 42 (int), accessing .Length throws RuntimeBinderException. Dynamic bypasses compile-time checks, deferring errors to runtime.

title: CancellationToken Ignored
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: CancellationToken is accepted but never checked. Task.Delay should receive the token: await Task.Delay(10, token). Also check token.IsCancellationRequested in the loop.

title: Array Covariance
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: C# arrays are covariant: string[] can be assigned to object[]. But writing a non-string value throws ArrayTypeMismatchException at runtime.

title: Recursive Property
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The property getter calls itself (Name instead of _name), creating infinite recursion → StackOverflowException. Should be 'return _name;' and '_name = value;'.

title: Global Using
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Global using directives (C# 10) apply to all files in the project. No need to repeat 'using System' in every file.

title: File-Scoped Namespace
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: File-scoped namespaces (C# 10) reduce indentation by declaring the namespace with a semicolon. All types in the file are in this namespace.

title: Pattern Matching with Property
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Property patterns (C# 8+) and extended property patterns with nested access (C# 10) allow matching on object properties concisely.

title: Required Members
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Required members (C# 11) enforce that properties must be set during initialization. Omitting them causes a compile error.

title: List Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: List patterns (C# 11) match collections by structure. '..' is the slice pattern matching zero or more elements.

title: Static Abstract Interface Members
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Static abstract interface members (C# 11) enable generic math. The interface constrains types to support the + operator.

title: Record Struct
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Record structs (C# 10) combine record benefits (value equality, ToString) with struct semantics (value type, stack allocated).

title: IAsyncEnumerable
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: IAsyncEnumerable (C# 8) enables async iteration with 'await foreach'. Each item is produced asynchronously.

title: With Expression on Record
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The 'with' expression (C# 9) creates a copy of a record with modified properties. dev has Host and Port changed but UseSSL stays true.

title: Implicit Usings
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: With implicit usings (C# 10 / .NET 6+), System, System.Collections.Generic, and System.Linq are automatically imported.

title: String Is Null Or Empty Methods
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: IsNullOrEmpty returns false for whitespace strings. IsNullOrWhiteSpace returns true. Output: False then True.

title: Async ValueTask
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: ValueTask avoids heap allocation when the result is already cached. For async paths, it wraps a regular Task.

title: Switch Expression with Tuple
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Switch expressions with tuple patterns (C# 8) and 'or' pattern combinator (C# 9) make complex matching concise.

title: Interpolated String Handler
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Format specifiers work in interpolated strings. D5 pads with zeros (00042), X converts to hex (2A). Output: 'The answer is 00042 and hex is 2A'.

title: Channel Producer-Consumer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: System.Threading.Channels provides high-performance producer-consumer patterns. ReadAllAsync returns IAsyncEnumerable for clean consumption.

title: Primary Constructor on Class
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Primary constructors on classes (C# 12) allow declaring constructor parameters inline. Parameters can initialize properties directly.

title: Collection Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Collection expressions (C# 12) use bracket syntax for initializing various collection types including List, arrays, and Span.

title: Default Interface Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Default interface methods (C# 8) provide default implementations. SimpleGreeter doesn't need to implement Greet — the default is used via the interface reference.

title: Static Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Static lambdas (C# 9) cannot capture local variables or 'this', avoiding unintentional allocation of closure objects.

title: Checked Arithmetic
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The 'checked' keyword enables overflow checking for arithmetic operations. Without it, int.MaxValue + 1 silently wraps around.

title: CallerMemberName Attribute
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: CallerMemberName automatically fills in the calling method name at compile time. Output: '[Main] Starting'.

title: Unsafe Fixed Buffer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Fixed-size buffers in unsafe structs allow inline array storage without heap allocation. Requires /unsafe compiler flag.

title: Sealed Class
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Sealed classes cannot be inherited. This can improve performance as the JIT can devirtualize calls. String and other framework types are sealed.

title: Parallel LINQ
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: PLINQ (AsParallel) parallelizes LINQ queries across multiple threads. For large datasets, this can significantly improve performance.

title: Ref Readonly
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Ref readonly returns (C# 7.2) return a reference to data without copying, while preventing modification. Useful for large structs.

title: Async Enumerable Without Cancellation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Infinite async enumerable without [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken parameter. The consumer has no way to cancel the infinite stream gracefully.

title: Finalizer Resurrection
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Re-registering an object for finalization in its own finalizer creates an immortal object that can never be garbage collected, causing memory leaks.

title: Volatile Not Enough
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: volatile ensures visibility but not atomicity. _counter++ is read-modify-write, which is not atomic. Use Interlocked.Increment for thread-safe incrementing.

title: Task.WhenAll Exception Handling
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: When using await on Task.WhenAll, only the first exception is thrown. The second is lost. Access task.Exception on each task to see all exceptions.

title: Lazy Initialization Race
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Double-checked locking issue without volatile or lock. Multiple threads can enter the null check simultaneously, creating multiple expensive objects. Use Lazy<T>.

title: Expression Tree Limitation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Expression trees cannot contain statement bodies (blocks with {}). They must be single expressions. Use 'x => x * 2 + 1' instead.

title: Struct in Dictionary Key
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Default struct Equals and GetHashCode use reflection, which is slow and may not produce correct hash codes. Implement IEquatable<Pair> for proper dictionary key behavior.

title: Event Unsubscribe Memory Leak
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The subscriber subscribes to DataChanged but never unsubscribes. The publisher holds a strong reference to the subscriber, preventing garbage collection — a memory leak.

title: GetAwaiter().GetResult() in UI
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: GetAwaiter().GetResult() blocks the calling thread. In a UI context with SynchronizationContext, this deadlocks because the async continuation needs the same thread.

title: Wrong Comparison Operator Overload
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Overloading == without also overloading != is a compile error. In C#, == and != must always be overloaded together.

title: Await in Catch Block
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While C# 6+ supports await in catch blocks, throwing after an await in a catch can behave differently than expected — the exception's stack trace may be modified.

title: Implicit Cast Precision Loss
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Implicit conversion from long to double can lose precision. double has ~15-17 significant digits. The comparison on line 5 may return true despite precision loss.

title: TaskCompletionSource Default Value
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The TaskCompletionSource never calls SetResult, SetException, or SetCanceled. The returned Task will never complete — await hangs forever.

title: Static Mutable State
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Static mutable state shared across threads without synchronization. Count++ is not atomic. Use Interlocked.Increment(ref Count) for thread safety.

title: Generic Constraint Missing
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot use 'new T()' without the 'where T : new()' constraint. The compiler doesn't know if T has a parameterless constructor.

title: HttpClient Instantiation in Loop
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Creating a new HttpClient per request exhausts sockets (port exhaustion). HttpClient should be reused or managed via IHttpClientFactory.

title: Incorrect Interface Disposal
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Process takes an IDisposable parameter (via IRepository) but doesn't dispose it. Either use 'using' or document the ownership contract clearly.

title: Incorrect Null Propagation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: head?.Next?.Next?.Value returns int? (nullable), but the method returns int. Cannot implicitly convert. Use ?? defaultValue or return int?.

title: Mutable Captured Variable in Task
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The variable 'i' is captured by reference in the lambda. By the time tasks execute, i may be 5 for all. Copy to a local variable inside the loop: var j = i;

title: Nullable Value Type Comparison
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: When both nullable values are null, relational comparisons (>=, <=, >, <) return false. Only == returns true for null == null. This is often surprising.

title: Span in Async Method
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Span<T> is a ref struct and cannot be used across await boundaries. The stack frame may not exist after the await resumes. Use Memory<T> instead.

title: Conditional Access in Assignment
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: list?.Add(42) does nothing when list is null — no exception but also no error indication. The developer likely expected list to be initialized. This silently swallows the bug.

title: SemaphoreSlim Without Release
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: If an exception occurs before Release(), the semaphore is never released — permanent deadlock for other callers. Wrap in try-finally: finally { _sem.Release(); }.

title: Struct Implementing Interface Boxing
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Passing a struct to a method accepting an interface parameter causes boxing (heap allocation). For performance-critical code, use generic constraints instead.

title: Multi-cast Delegate Return Value
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Multi-cast delegates with return values only return the last delegate's result. combined() returns 2, silently discarding 1. This is rarely the intended behavior.

title: String.Create
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: String.Create allocates a string and fills it via a Span<char> — zero-copy string creation. Output: 'ABCDE'.

title: Conditional Weak Reference
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: WeakReference<T> doesn't prevent garbage collection. After obj = null and GC.Collect(), TryGetTarget likely returns false.

title: Throw Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Throw expressions (C# 7) can be used in null-coalescing, conditional, and expression-bodied members for concise null validation.

title: Generic Host Builder
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Generic Host (.NET Core 3+) provides DI, configuration, and logging. AddSingleton registers a service with singleton lifetime.

title: Stackalloc in Span
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: stackalloc with Span<T> (C# 7.2) allocates on the stack, avoiding GC pressure. Output: '0 1 4 9 16'.

title: When Clause in Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Relational patterns with 'and' combinator (C# 9) create readable range checks. 85 matches the B range.

title: Inline Array
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Inline arrays (C# 12) create fixed-length buffer types with indexer support. The struct stores 4 ints inline without heap allocation.

title: Deconstruct Extension Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Deconstruct can be an extension method. This enables tuple-style deconstruction of KeyValuePair in foreach loops.

title: Covariant Interface
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: IEnumerable<T> is covariant (out T). A List<Dog> can be passed where IEnumerable<Animal> is expected because Dog extends Animal.

title: String Interpolation Newline
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Raw string literals combined with interpolation (C# 11) allow multi-line templates with expressions. No escaping needed.

title: Frozen Collections
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: FrozenDictionary (.NET 8) creates an immutable, optimized-for-read dictionary. Lookups are faster than regular Dictionary for static data.

title: Abstract Static Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Static abstract methods in interfaces (C# 11) enable calling static methods generically. T.Parse() dispatches to the correct implementation.

title: Interceptors Preview
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is basic valid C# code. It demonstrates a simple expression-bodied method returning a string. Output: 'Hello, World!'.

title: Params Span
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: params ReadOnlySpan<T> (C# 13) avoids heap allocation for params. The arguments are passed via stack-allocated span instead of an array.

title: Task.CompletedTask
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Task.CompletedTask provides a cached, already-completed task. Avoids creating a new Task when the method completes synchronously.

title: Immutable Collections
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: ImmutableList.Add returns a new list. The original is unchanged. list.Count = 3, newList.Count = 4. Thread-safe by design.

title: Nested Pattern Matching
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Extended property patterns (C# 10) allow nested property access. Both styles — Home.Country and { City: } — work for matching nested properties.

title: Ref Struct
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Ref structs are stack-only types that can contain Span<T>. They cannot be boxed, stored in arrays, or used as fields in regular classes.

title: PeriodicTimer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: PeriodicTimer (.NET 6) provides async-friendly periodic timing. WaitForNextTickAsync is awaitable and works well with cancellation tokens.

title: Generic Math
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Generic math (C# 11 / .NET 7) with INumber<T> enables writing type-agnostic math. T.Zero and T.CreateChecked use static interface members.

title: String Comparison Culture
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: ß (German sharp s) equals SS in German culture but not in ordinal comparison. Ordinal: False, Cultural (German): True. Culture matters!

title: Abstract Record
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Records can be abstract and support inheritance. They combine value semantics with polymorphism. Circle gets value equality automatically.

title: Spread Operator in Collections
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The spread operator (..) in collection expressions (C# 12) flattens collections into another. combined = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8].

title: Alias Any Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Using aliases for any type (C# 12) enables naming tuples, arrays, and other types. 'using Point = (int X, int Y)' creates a readable alias.

title: Lock Object (.NET 9)
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: System.Threading.Lock (.NET 9 / C# 13) is a dedicated lock type that's lighter than using object. The lock statement recognizes it specially.

title: Incorrect IDisposable Pattern with Sealed
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Dispose() calls GC.SuppressFinalize but never actually releases the unmanaged resource (_handle). The handle is left dangling.

title: Parallel.ForEach with Shared State
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: sum += i is not thread-safe. Multiple threads reading and writing sum concurrently causes data races. Use Interlocked.Add or Parallel.For with local init.

title: JsonSerializer Circular Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: TreeNode has circular references (Parent ↔ Children). JsonSerializer throws JsonException for cycles by default. Use ReferenceHandler.Preserve or [JsonIgnore].

title: Incorrect ValueTask Consumption
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A ValueTask must not be awaited more than once. The second await is undefined behavior. Store the result in a variable after the first await.

title: Exception Filter Side Effect
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Exception filters with side effects are dangerous. The filter may be evaluated multiple times during stack unwinding, incrementing attempts unpredictably.

title: Record Mutation Confusion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 'with' does a shallow copy. guest.Roles and admin.Roles reference the same List. Adding 'guest' to guest's roles also modifies admin's roles.

title: Implicit Interface on Struct Copy
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Passing struct to interface parameter boxes it. Increment modifies the boxed copy. c.Count in Main is still 0. Output: 1 then 0.

title: ConfigureServices Order
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Second registration replaces the first for single-resolve. GetRequiredService returns ServiceB, not ServiceA. Use GetServices<IService>() to get all registrations.

title: Async Dispose Without Using
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: AsyncResource implements IAsyncDisposable but is never disposed. Use 'await using var res = new AsyncResource()' for proper async disposal.

title: Enum Flags Without Attribute
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing [Flags] attribute. While bitwise operations work, ToString() shows '3' instead of 'Read, Write'. Add [Flags] for proper string representation.

title: Task.Run in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Starting a background task in a constructor is problematic: no cancellation support, unobserved exceptions, and the object may not be fully initialized when the task starts.

title: ConcurrentDictionary Race
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: ContainsKey + set is not atomic, even with ConcurrentDictionary. Use AddOrUpdate or GetOrAdd for thread-safe compound operations.

title: Property Changed Without Notification
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Name setter doesn't raise PropertyChanged event. Data binding won't update the UI when Name changes. Call OnPropertyChanged(nameof(Name)) after setting.

title: Wrong Async State Machine
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Not thread-safe: ContainsKey + index is not atomic. Multiple threads can start parallel DB loads for the same key. Use ConcurrentDictionary.GetOrAdd with Lazy<Task<string>>.

title: String Pool Confusion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Literal strings are interned, but 'new string(...)' creates a new object. ReferenceEquals returns false even though == returns true. This confuses reference vs value equality.

title: Incorrect Async Void Lambda
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: An async lambda assigned to Action becomes async void. The exception on line 6 terminates the process — it can't be caught. Use Func<Task> instead.

title: Incorrect Record Inheritance
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A class cannot inherit from a record. Records can only inherit from other records. Use 'record Derived(...) : Base(...)' instead.

title: Awaiting Null ValueTask
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: result is null when condition is false. Accessing .Value on a null Nullable<ValueTask<string>> throws InvalidOperationException. Check HasValue first.

title: Unsafe String Mutation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Strings are immutable. Modifying via unsafe pointer corrupts the interned string pool. Both s and the literal 'Hello' may show 'Jello' — undefined behavior.

title: Missing GetHashCode in Record Override
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Overriding Equals on a record without overriding GetHashCode breaks the equality contract. a == b is true but they may have different hash codes, causing dictionary issues.

title: Async Enumerator Without Disposal
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: IAsyncEnumerator implements IAsyncDisposable. Not disposing it via 'await using' can leak resources. Use 'await foreach' instead for automatic disposal.

title: Static Constructor Exception
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A static constructor runs once. If it throws, the type becomes permanently unusable — TypeInitializationException on every access. The second access also fails.

title: Equality After Downcast
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Base.Equals checks only Id and uses 'is Base' (not exact type check). Two different Derived instances with different Names compare as equal. Derived should override Equals.

title: Incorrect Middleware Ordering
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: ToUpper runs before Trim. '  hello  ' becomes '  HELLO  ' then 'HELLO'. But the intent was likely Trim first then ToUpper. Middleware order matters!

title: Dispose in Finalizer Thread
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: In the finalizer, _writer may already be finalized (GC order is non-deterministic for managed objects). Accessing it can throw or corrupt state. Only release unmanaged resources in finalizers.

title: Task.WhenAny Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Task.WhenAny returns the first completed task. The 100ms task wins. Output: 'fast'. The slow task continues but its result is ignored.

title: Utf8 String Literal
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: UTF-8 string literals (C# 11) create ReadOnlySpan<byte> directly from string syntax. No encoding conversion needed at runtime.

title: Interface Contravariance
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Contravariance (in T) allows assigning IProcessor<object> to IProcessor<string>. An object processor can handle strings since string IS an object.

title: Async Streams with Cancellation
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: EnumeratorCancellation (C# 8) properly passes CancellationToken to async enumerators. The stream stops after ~500ms.

title: Source Generator Output
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Source generators use attributes to mark classes for code generation. The partial keyword allows the generated code to extend the class. Valid pattern.

title: Lazy<T> Thread Safe
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Lazy<T> with ExecutionAndPublication ensures thread-safe, single initialization. Only one thread creates the instance; others wait.

title: Unsafe Span Cast
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: MemoryMarshal.AsBytes reinterprets memory. 4 ints × 4 bytes = 16 bytes. Output: 'ints: 4, bytes: 16'. Zero-copy type reinterpretation.

title: String.GetHashCode Stability
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: While valid, note that string.GetHashCode() returns different values across app domains and process restarts in .NET Core. Don't persist hash codes.

title: Pattern Matching Not
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Pattern combinators 'not' and 'or' (C# 9) enable expressive checks. 's is not (null or "")' is cleaner than !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s).

title: Nullable Operator Cascade
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Null-conditional (?.) chains and null-coalescing (??) combine safely. When config is null, the entire chain short-circuits. Output: 'DEFAULT'.

title: Natural Type Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Natural type for lambdas (C# 10) allows 'var' to infer delegate types. The compiler infers Func<int,int,int> and Func<string>.

title: CancellationToken Registration
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: CancellationToken.Register adds a callback that fires on cancellation. The registration is disposable. Output: 'Cancelled!'.

title: Ranges in Patterns
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Relational patterns work with char comparisons using ASCII ordering. 'G' matches uppercase, '7' matches digit.

title: File-Scoped Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: File-scoped types (C# 11) are visible only within the declaring file. They're useful for source generator helpers and internal implementation details.

title: ArrayPool for Efficiency
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: ArrayPool<T> rents and returns arrays to avoid GC pressure. Rent may return a larger array. Always return rented arrays in finally blocks.

title: Record With Validation
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Records can have custom constructors with validation. This creates a value object pattern where the Email is validated on creation.

title: SemaphoreSlim Async
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: SemaphoreSlim(3) limits concurrency to 3 simultaneous accessors. WaitAsync + finally Release is the proper pattern.

title: Minimal API
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Minimal APIs (.NET 6+) allow defining endpoints without controllers. Route parameters are automatically bound from the URL.

title: Checked User-Defined Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Checked user-defined operators (C# 11) allow types to define separate checked/unchecked behavior for arithmetic operations.

title: TimeProvider Abstraction
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: TimeProvider (.NET 8) abstracts time for testability. TimeProvider.System uses real time; FakeTimeProvider can be used in tests.

title: InterpolatedStringHandler Custom
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is basic valid C# with string interpolation. Custom InterpolatedStringHandler can optimize conditional logging to avoid string allocation.

title: Conditional Access Assignment
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: ??= assigns only if left side is null. First ??= assigns {1,2,3}. Second doesn't because arr is no longer null. Output: 3, 3.

title: Task.FromCanceled
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Task.FromCanceled creates an already-canceled task. Awaiting it throws TaskCanceledException. Useful for early cancellation without async overhead.

title: Exception Deconstruction
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Exceptions can have Deconstruct methods for tuple-style deconstruction in catch blocks. This provides clean error information extraction.

title: Discriminated Union with OneOf
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested records simulate discriminated unions. Pattern matching exhaustively handles each case. This is a common functional pattern in C#.
